zh.lifeSummary: 當Ralph le Rouse於大約1285年出生在英國英格蘭戴文時，他的父親Roger le Rous50歲，母親Alianore Avenbury49歲。他於1298年與Joan Godisford在英國英格蘭戴文易維布利其結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1326年在英國英格蘭戴文易維布利其享年42歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭戴文易維布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ralph le Rouse于大约1285年出生在英国英格兰戴文时，他的父亲Roger le Rous50岁，母亲Alianore Avenbury49岁。他于1298年与Joan Godisford在英国英格兰戴文易維布利其结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1326年在英国英格兰戴文易維布利其享年42岁，埋葬在英国英格兰戴文易維布利其。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Rouse
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a person with red hair or a ruddy complexion, from Middle English, Anglo-Norman French rous(e) ‘red(-haired)’ (from Latin russ(e)us).
Americanized form of German   Raus  .
Walloon and northern French (Rousé): variant of Rosé (see Rose ).

variants.name: Ruse
meanings: English: variant of   Rouse  . Compare   Rowse  .
German: variant of Reusse (see Reuss 1).
Probably also an Americanized form of Czech   Rus   ‘Russian’.

variants.name: Russell
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: of Norman origin, from Old French and Anglo-Norman French r(o)ussel, a diminutive of Old French rous(e) ‘red, reddish’, used either as a nickname for someone with red hair or a ruddy complexion, or as a personal name. Compare Rouse . This Norman name has been established in Ireland since the 12th century. It has been reinforced in Britain and Ireland by Huguenot bearers of the name Roussel, of the same Old French origin.

English: habitational name from any of several places called Rushall (Norfolk, Staffordshire, Wiltshire) or possibly sometimes from Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent). Rushall in Staffordshire derives from Old English rysc ‘rush, rushes’ + halh ‘nook, corner of land’. Rushall in Norfolk derives from an uncertain first element + Old English halh. Rushall in Wiltshire derives from an Old English personal name Rust (genitive Rustes) + halh. Rusthall in Speldhurst (Kent) probably derives from Old English rust ‘rust, rust color’ + wella ‘well, spring, stream’, but with a change in the final element due to influence from Middle English, Old English hall ‘hall, residence’, perhaps referring to a nearby building.
Americanized form of German Rüssel, from a pet form of any of various personal names formed with the element hrōd ‘fame, renown’.

variants.name: Russ
meanings: English: variant of   Rouse  .
German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with hrōd ‘renown’, for example Rudolf.
German (of Slavic origin): from Old Slavic rusъ ‘reddish’, used as a nickname (compare 6 below and Rus ).

variants.name: Raus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Harro.
German: nickname for a noisy or raucous person, from Middle High German rūz ‘noise’.

variants.name: Rowse
meanings: English: variant of   Rouse  .
Cornish: habitational name from Rowse in Pillaton (Cornwall). The placename derives from Middle Cornish ros ‘promontory, hill spur, moor’.

variants.name: Rowsey
meanings: Altered form of a Walloon or perhaps French (mainly northern) surname, probably Rouzé or Rousé (see Rouse ), variants of Rosé (see Rose ). The surname Rowsey was brought to North America from England. Compare Rousey and Rowzee .

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Ralph le Rouse was born about 1285, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Roger le Rous, was 50 and his mother, Alianore Avenbury, was 49. He married Joan Godisford in 1298, in Ivybridge, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died in 1326, in Ivybridge, Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 42, and was buried in Ivybridge, Devon, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ralph le Rouse est né environ 1285, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Roger le Rous, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Alianore Avenbury, avait 49 ans. Il a épousé Joan Godisford en 1298, à Ivybridge, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1326, à Ivybridge, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 42 ans, et a été enterré à Ivybridge, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ralph le Rouse um 1285 in Devon, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger le Rous 50 und seine Mutter Alianore Avenbury 49. Er hat 1298 in Ivybridge, Devon, England, United Kingdom Joan Godisford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1326 im Alter von 42 Jahren in Ivybridge, Devon, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Ivybridge, Devon, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph le Rouse è nato il circa 1285 nel Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Roger le Rous, aveva 50 anni e il suo madre, Alianore Avenbury, aveva 49 anni. Lui sposato Joan Godisford nel 1298, nel Ivybridge, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1326, nel Ivybridge, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 42 anni, e fu sepolto a Ivybridge, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Ralph le Rouseが約1285年にイギリスイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父Roger le Rousは50歳，母Alianore Avenburyは49歳であった。1298年にイギリスイギリスデボンアイvイブリッジでJoan Godisfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1326年にイギリスイギリスデボンアイvイブリッジで42歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスデボンアイvイブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ralph le Rouse(이)가 대략 1285년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger le Rous(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Alianore Avenbury(은)는 49세였다. 그는 1298년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 이비브리지에서 Joan Godisford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1326년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 이비브리지에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 이비브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ralph le Rouse nasceu aproximadamente 1285, em Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Roger le Rous, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Alianore Avenbury, tinha 49 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Godisford em 1298, em Ivybridge, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1326, em Ivybridge, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 42 anos, e foi sepultado em Ivybridge, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ralph le Rouse родился около 1285 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger le Rous было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Alianore Avenbury, 49. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Godisford в 1298 г. в местности ивибридж, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1326 г. в местности ивибридж, девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 42 лет и был похоронен в местности ивибридж, девон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ralph le Rouse nació aproximadamente 1285, en Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Roger le Rous, tenía 50 años y su mamá, Alianore Avenbury, tenía 49 años. Se casó con Joan Godisford en 1298, en Ivybridge, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1326, en Ivybridge, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 42 años, y fue sepultado en Ivybridge, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
